title: P0 child: isolate Hetzner-Supabase 502 and add safe worker-only restart path

sha_binding.verified_source_note: PR #1256 (read-only diagnostic, head 6f142c94) merged to main as e2e3fa14. This continuation, still on the same branch/lane, adds: (1) two diagnostic gap fixes to ops-network-diagnose.yml (DB/pooler variable-name discovery by pattern instead of a fixed legacy name list; an in-container Node https fallback probe for when curl is unavailable in the worker image, sending no authorization header so the real claim_next_outbox RPC is never invoked with production credentials), and (2) a new manual-dispatch-only ops-worker-recovery.yml, prepared for PM review and NOT dispatched by this lane. No production code, migration, or delivery-target config touched.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Not independent Griff review, t1-approved, or a pm-verdict/v1 owner artifact.

r_level.command: pnpm exec tsx scripts/ci/r-level-check.ts --base origin/main --head HEAD

workflow_safety_review.note: Self-review of ops-network-diagnose.yml against the issue's explicit guardrails (do not mutate queues/delivery targets/env/containers; never print secret values):

checks.check: Only SSH commands executed are read-only: getent, dig, curl, openssl s_client, docker network ls/inspect, docker logs, ip route/link, sudo iptables -L / ufw status (best-effort, read-only flags only)
No docker restart/stop/rm/exec-with-write, no sed/tee/> against .env.production or any config file, no queue/DB write commands

Secret values (SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, DB passwords) are never echoed -- only hostnames/ports are parsed out of SUPABASE_URL/DATABASE_URL-style values via sed/cut, and the DB test is a bare TCP connect (/dev/tcp) with no credentials sent
UNIT_TALK_DISCORD_TARGET_MAP is never read or written by this workflow
Workflow trigger is workflow_dispatch only -- no automatic/scheduled execution, no trigger on push/PR events

codex_review_fixes.finding: HTTPS reachability checks probed the bare host root (https://$REST_HOST/) instead of the actual PostgREST route (/rest/v1/) the failing outbox claim calls use, which could pass while the real route still 502s
codex_review_fixes.fix: Steps 3-4 now probe https://$REST_HOST/rest/v1/ on host (IPv4/IPv6) and inside the worker container

codex_review_fixes.finding: The worker's configured target was read via `grep -m1 worker.startup`, which returns the OLDEST match if the container restarted more than once, misreporting a stale pre-restart target as current
codex_review_fixes.fix: Step 10 now uses `grep worker.startup | tail -1` to read the most recent startup line

runtime_proof.note: Live smoke run per T1 policy; this lane changes only a new GitHub Actions workflow file and its own proof bundle, no production code path or migration.

queries.description: INSERT via processSubmission — one row per synthetic pick, in the 6 of 7 database-smoke tests that create a pick
INSERT via processSubmission (same 6 tests); UPDATE lifecycle_state via transitionPickLifecycle (queued, posted) and via the settlement RPC path

INSERT on each successful transitionPickLifecycle call and on each successful settlement transition; SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests to assert zero rows were written for a specific to_state after a rejected atomic operation

INSERT via one successful enqueueDistributionAtomic call; UPDATE status to 'processing' via claimNextAtomic; a separate enqueue attempt is rejected and asserted via findByIdempotencyKey returning null
SELECT COUNT(*) used to assert zero rows were written after a rejected confirmDeliveryAtomic call

INSERT via recordPickSettlement/settlePickAtomic on successful calls; one settlePickAtomic call is rejected and asserted via SELECT COUNT(*) returning zero; SELECT used to assert exactly one base record and that a correction does not mutate the original row
SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests to assert zero audit rows for a rejected atomic operation, and by one test to assert at least two audit rows exist for a successful settlement plus its correction
read-only SELECT (listByType('player')) scanning the full live table for the duplicate (external_id, sport, participant_type) invariant

post_merge_findings.dispatched_run: https://github.com/griff843/Unit-Talk-v2/actions (ops-network-diagnose.yml, run against main after PR #1256 merged)
post_merge_findings.summary: Host DNS, container DNS, IPv4 REST GET /rest/v1/ (HTTP/2 401, cloudflare, cf-ray present), TLS handshake, Docker network state, and route/MTU/firewall all healthy. IPv6 REST unavailable only because no AAAA record exists for the domain (not a fault). Container curl probe and DB/pooler TCP test were both gaps in the original workflow -- fixed in this continuation (see acceptance_criteria). Worker log tail shows a real Cloudflare 502 HTML error page and a separate 'canceling statement due to statement timeout' error on the claim_next_outbox RPC path. Bounded conclusion: intermittent Cloudflare-edge/PostgREST RPC-path failure, not a Hetzner-side DNS/TLS/routing/firewall/Docker fault -- every host-level network primitive checked out healthy at diagnostic time.

known_limitations: The safe worker-only restart mechanism is now added (ops-worker-recovery.yml) but is manual-dispatch-only and has NOT been dispatched by this lane -- PM review of the workflow file is required before any dispatch, per the issue's explicit instruction not to restart production yet.

docker events window in the diagnostic workflows generally is bounded (host-dependent daemon log retention); this does not affect the worker-recovery workflow's own pre/post state capture, which reads live .State/.RestartCount directly.

acceptance_criteria.db_pooler_endpoint_tested: PASS -- step 6, TCP-connect only, no credentials
acceptance_criteria.host_firewall_egress_state_captured: PASS (best-effort, non-root SSH user may lack sudo) -- step 9
acceptance_criteria.worker_actual_configured_target_confirme: PASS -- step 10, reads the live worker.startup log line rather than assuming discord:canary/discord:best-bets
acceptance_criteria.db_pooler_variable_name_discovered_by_pa: PASS -- step 0 now pattern-matches *DB*URL*/*POOL*/*CONNECTION* key NAMES only, falls back to the legacy fixed list only if no pattern match
acceptance_criteria.in_container_probe_works_when_curl_absen: PASS -- step 4 falls back to a Node https.request GET with no authorization header when curl is not on the worker image's PATH
acceptance_criteria.no_production_claim_rpc_invoked_with_rea: PASS -- both the curl and Node fallback probes are unauthenticated GET requests against the same route; no Authorization/apikey header is ever sent
acceptance_criteria.narrow_worker_only_recovery_workflow_add: PASS -- ops-worker-recovery.yml touches unit-talk-worker-1 only, workflow_dispatch-only with an explicit typed confirmation input
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_no_env_target_deploy_p: PASS -- the only mutating command in the entire file is a single `docker restart unit-talk-worker-1`
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_captures_pre_post_stat: PASS -- image/restart-count/target/health/logs captured both before and after
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_aborts_if_502_conditio: PASS -- greps the last 200 log lines for the 502/claim-failure signature and exits 0 with no restart if the count is zero
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_one_restart_attempt_ma: PASS -- exactly one `docker restart` call in the whole script, no retry loop around it
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_not_dispatched_by_this: PASS -- prepared and committed only; returned for PM review before any dispatch

owner_boundary.pm_verdict_v1: present_and_consumed -- fresh Griff-authored PM_VERDICT: APPROVED posted 2026-07-19T07:57:24Z on PR #1256, exact head 6f142c9483823c969b52e446b8f4824cdaad6553 (the merged head), explicitly scoping approval to the workflow-dispatch read-only diagnostic and its lane/proof metadata only -- not authorizing restart/deploy/env mutation/target remap/queue replay/DB write
owner_boundary.note: PR #1256's merge authority was satisfied by both required T1 artifacts: the exact-head pm-verdict/v1 APPROVED comment and the t1-approved label. This continuation (network-diagnose gap fixes + the new worker-recovery workflow) is a SEPARATE change requiring its own fresh exact-head verdict before merge -- the #1256 verdict does not carry forward.
